description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain texture and a darkened finish. The head of the axe is made of metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end. The blade has a sharp, beveled edge and a rectangular profile, suitable for chopping or splitting wood. The handle is ergonomically shaped for grip, tapering slightly toward the end, and includes a small metal ring or lanyard hole at the base. The mounting of the blade to the handle appears traditional, with the head fitted securely onto the top of the handle. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian tool, likely used for woodcutting or similar tasks in urban or suburban environments.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, sharp metal blade, designed for chopping tasks. The axe features a traditional mounting and a natural wood finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow edge of the blade and the alignment of the handle directly behind it.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other face of the blade and the handle's curvature, similar to view 0 but mirrored.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat poll of the axe head and the handle extending directly backward.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and a lanyard hole at the base.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a beveled edge and a flat poll; rectangular profile with no visible beard.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the top of the wooden handle.
